title: Cider Crunchy Slaw
description: A fresh, silky Australian dish featuring cider vinegar, made to be enjoyed as a low-carb-friendly snack or side during sick days.
instructions: Wash the lettuce leaves thoroughly. Cut off the bottom part of each leaf, keeping them whole if desired.
Gently heat a small amount of water in a pressure cooker or saucepan until it reaches a simmering point, about 180°F (82°C). Do not let the water boil.
Place the chopped celery and onion gently into the hot water to soften. Cook for approximately 3-4 minutes over medium heat. Remove from heat immediately after cooking time ends.

In a large mixing bowl, combine the softened vegetables with the thinly sliced fennel, chopped parsley, cider vinegar, olive oil, salt, and pepper. Mix well until all ingredients are evenly distributed.
If serving as a snack or side dish, let the mixture cool slightly before transferring it to plates or bowls for presentation.
